title: Batch 3 & 4: Grouped display layout with expandable rows, ageing badges, image modal, and filters/search across tabs

requirements.summary: Transform Total Orders tab to display grouped, expandable design code rows with ageing badges, karigar name, order type badges, and hierarchical selection
acceptanceCriteria: Orders are visually grouped by design code with a collapsible group header row
Group header shows design code, order type badge (RB/CO/SO), karigar name, sub-order count, qty, and weight
Chevron icon toggles expand/collapse of sub-orders for that group
Sub-order rows show order number, qty, weight, generic name, order date, and ageing badge
Ageing badge is green for ≤14 days, amber for 15–29 days, red for ≥30 days
Group checkbox selects/deselects all sub-orders in the group
Individual sub-order checkboxes work independently
Mark as Ready action works correctly on selected sub-orders

file_operations.description: Replace the existing OrderTable with a new grouped layout that groups orders by design code. Implement collapsible group rows with a chevron toggle. Each group header displays: design code (clickable), order type badge (RB/CO/SO derived from first order in group), karigar name, total sub-order count, aggregate qty sum, and aggregate weight sum. Include a checkbox on the group header that selects/deselects all sub-orders in that group. Sub-order rows (shown when expanded) display: order number, qty, weight, generic name, order date, and an ageing badge computed as days since order date (green ≤14d, amber 15–29d, red ≥30d). Each sub-order has its own checkbox. Preserve the existing Mark as Ready action and SuppliedQtyDialog for RB orders. Manage expansion state using React state (e.g., a Set of expanded design codes).

Create a reusable AgeingBadge component that accepts an order date (Time bigint) and computes the number of days since that date. Return a badge with the text '{days}d' and background color: green (bg-green-100 text-green-800 dark:bg-green-900 dark:text-green-300) for ≤14 days, amber (bg-amber-100 text-amber-800 dark:bg-amber-900 dark:text-amber-300) for 15–29 days, red (bg-red-100 text-red-800 dark:bg-red-900 dark:text-red-300) for ≥30 days. Use Tailwind classes and shadcn/ui Badge component for consistent styling.

requirements.summary: Apply the same grouped design code layout with expandable sub-orders, ageing badges, karigar name, and order type badges to Ready and Hallmark tabs
acceptanceCriteria: Ready tab groups orders by design code with expandable sub-orders, ageing badges, karigar name, and order type badge
Hallmark tab groups orders by design code with expandable sub-orders, ageing badges, karigar name, and order type badge
Existing action buttons (return to pending, mark as returned) remain functional on sub-order rows or group rows as appropriate

file_operations.description: Replace the existing table with the same grouped design code layout implemented in TotalOrdersTab. Group ready orders by design code, display group headers with design code (clickable), order type badge, karigar name, sub-order count, aggregate qty, and aggregate weight. Include a chevron toggle for expand/collapse and a group checkbox. Sub-order rows show order number, qty, weight, generic name, order date, and ageing badge (reusing the AgeingBadge component). Preserve the existing 'return to pending' action (delete mutation) on individual sub-orders. Manage expansion state per-group.

Replace the existing table with the same grouped design code layout. Group hallmark orders by design code, display group headers with design code (clickable), order type badge, karigar name, sub-order count, aggregate qty, and aggregate weight. Include a chevron toggle and group checkbox. Sub-order rows show order number, qty, weight, generic name, order date, and ageing badge. Preserve the existing filters (order type, karigar, date range, search text) and the 'mark as returned' bulk action. Ensure the filters operate on the grouped data and the bulk selection applies across expanded sub-orders. Manage expansion state per-group.

requirements.summary: Enable design code click in group headers to open DesignImageModal across Total Orders, Ready, and Hallmark tabs
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking a design code text in the group header opens DesignImageModal for that design code
Modal shows the design image with zoom and pan (existing DesignImageModal behaviour)
Modal can be closed and the table remains in its current expand/collapse state
Works across Total Orders, Ready, and Hallmark tabs

file_operations.description: Add state to track the currently selected design code for the image modal (e.g., const [selectedDesignCode, setSelectedDesignCode] = useState<string | null>(null)). In the group header row, wrap the design code text in a clickable element (button or link styled as text) that calls setSelectedDesignCode(designCode) on click. Render the existing DesignImageModal component at the bottom of the component, passing selectedDesignCode and an onClose handler that resets selectedDesignCode to null. Ensure the modal does not interfere with expansion state.

Add state to track the currently selected design code for the image modal. In the group header row, make the design code clickable to open DesignImageModal. Render DesignImageModal at the bottom of the component with selectedDesignCode and onClose handler. Preserve expansion state when modal opens and closes.

Add state to track the currently selected design code for the image modal. In the group header row, make the design code clickable to open DesignImageModal. Render DesignImageModal at the bottom of the component with selectedDesignCode and onClose handler. Preserve expansion state when modal opens and closes.

requirements.summary: Add search bar and two filter dropdowns (order type, karigar) above the orders table in Total Orders, Ready, and Hallmark tabs
acceptanceCriteria: Search bar is present above the orders table in Total Orders, Ready, and Hallmark tabs
Typing in the search bar filters groups/sub-orders by design code, order number, or generic name (partial, case-insensitive)
Order type dropdown offers options: All Types, RB, CO, SO
Karigar dropdown is populated with unique karigar names from current orders, plus an 'All Karigars' default
Selecting an order type hides groups that do not match
Selecting a karigar hides groups not assigned to that karigar
All three controls combine with AND logic
Filters reset when switching tabs
Layout matches the screenshot: search left, two dropdowns right

file_operations.description: Add state for search text, selected order type filter (All Types | RB | CO | SO), and selected karigar filter (All Karigars | specific karigar name). Derive the list of unique karigars from the orders data. Above the grouped table, render a flex container with: a search input (shadcn/ui Input) on the left with placeholder 'Search by order no, design code, generic name', and two shadcn/ui Select dropdowns on the right styled as pill/capsule buttons (one for order type, one for karigar). Apply filters to the orders data before grouping: filter by search text (match design code, order number, or generic name case-insensitively), filter by order type if not 'All Types', filter by karigar name if not 'All Karigars'. Use AND logic. Reset filters to default on component mount or when tab changes.

Add state for search text, order type filter, and karigar filter. Derive unique karigars from ready orders. Above the grouped table, render the same filter UI: search input on the left, two pill-style Select dropdowns (order type, karigar) on the right. Apply filters to ready orders before grouping using AND logic (search text matches design code/order number/generic name, order type matches if not 'All Types', karigar matches if not 'All Karigars'). Reset filters on component mount.

HallmarkTab already has filters (order type, karigar, date range, search text). Refactor the existing filter UI to match the new layout: search input on the left, order type and karigar dropdowns on the right (styled as pills). Ensure the filters combine with AND logic and apply to the grouped data. Preserve the existing date range filter and export functionality. Reset filters on component mount.
